evals.prompt: I'm building a Kotlin backend service that needs to fetch user profiles from a database, enrich them with data from 3 external APIs (preferences, activity, recommendations), and return the combined result. Each external API call takes 200-500ms. Design a coroutine-based service layer with proper structured concurrency, timeout handling (2 second deadline for the whole operation), and graceful degradation (return partial data if some enrichment calls fail).
evals.expected_output: A coroutine-based service using supervisorScope for parallel enrichment calls with individual timeouts, a composed deadline via withTimeout, and result types that model partial success.
assertions: Uses supervisorScope or async with SupervisorJob for parallel enrichment calls so that one failure does not cancel siblings
Applies withTimeout or withTimeoutOrNull for the overall 2-second deadline and individual call timeouts
Uses a sealed class or Result type to model partial success where some enrichment data may be missing
Dispatches I/O calls on Dispatchers.IO using withContext and does not block the calling dispatcher
Includes structured error handling that does not catch CancellationException (or explicitly rethrows it)

evals.prompt: I'm starting a Kotlin Multiplatform project that shares a domain layer between an Android app and an iOS app. The shared module needs: a data model with sealed types for order states, a repository interface with Flow-based reactive queries, kotlinx.serialization for API responses, and expect/actual for platform-specific logging. Show me the module structure and key implementations.
evals.expected_output: A KMP module structure with commonMain containing sealed domain types, repository interface returning Flow, serialization models, and expect/actual declarations, plus androidMain and iosMain actual implementations.
assertions: Defines the shared module with commonMain, androidMain, and iosMain source sets in the Gradle configuration
Uses sealed class or sealed interface in commonMain for order state modeling with data class variants
Defines a repository interface in commonMain returning Flow<T> for reactive data access
Uses @Serializable annotations from kotlinx.serialization for API response models in commonMain
Implements expect/actual pattern for platform-specific logging (expect in commonMain, actual in androidMain and iosMain)
